- ww at cols 40-41: white background
Wait, the G at cols 28-31 is dark gray, not charcoal. This is strange. The left blue square was at cols 29-30 (b), and its border was charcoal (c). After the SPACE shot, the charcoal border became dark gray (G)?
Actually, looking more carefully:
- GGGG at cols 28-31, rows 24-27: this is a 4x4 dark gray square
- b at cols 29-30, rows 25-26: blue square inside the dark gray square
- W at col 32, rows 24-27: W bar
- NNN at cols 33-35, rows 24-27: player
So the left blue square's border changed from charcoal (c) to dark gray (G). This is different from the bottom frame, which changed from charcoal (c) to white (W).
Wait, but the bottom frame was at rows 36-39, cols 24-27. After the SPACE shot, it became white. But now I see a dark gray square at cols 28-31, rows 24-27. This is a different object.
Actually, I think I'm confusing things. Let me re-examine the segmentation:
- b: rows 25-26, cols 29-30 (blue square)
- W: rows 24-27, cols 32-32 (W bar)
- N: rows 24-27, cols 33-35 (player)
The dark gray (G) square is not in the segmentation. Let me check if it's part of another object.
Actually, looking at the ascii again:
Row 24: `GGGGWNNNwwwwww`
Row 25: `GbbGWNNNwwwwww`
Row 26: `GbbGWNNNwwwwww`
Row 27: `GGGGWNNNwwwwww`
The G at cols 28-31 forms a 4x4 hollow square (border only), with blue (b) at cols 29-30 inside. This looks like the left blue square's border, which was originally charcoal (c) but now is dark gray (G).
Wait, but the segmentation doesn't show a G object. Let me check if the G is part of the middle frame or something else.
Actually, I think the G is the middle frame's border, which is blue (b), not dark gray (G). Let me re-examine.
Hmm, the segmentation shows:
- b: rows 28-31, cols 28-39 (blue frame)
- g: rows 29-30, cols 29-38 (gray inside)
So the middle frame is at rows 28-31, not rows 24-27. The G at rows 24-27, cols 28-31 is a different object.
Wait, but the segmentation doesn't show a G object at rows 24-27, cols 28-31. Let me check the segmentation again.
Actually, I think the G is the left blue square's border, which was originally charcoal (c) but changed to dark gray (G) after the SPACE shot. But the segmentation doesn't show it as a separate object because it's connected to something else.
Actually, looking at the segmentation output:
- c: rows 24-27, cols 44-47 (charcoal frame, unconverted)
- b: rows 25-26, cols 29-30 (blue square, left of player)
- b: rows 25-26, cols 45-46 (blue square inside top-right frame)
The left blue square at cols 29-30 is still there, but its border changed from charcoal to dark gray. This is strange.
Wait, I think I need to reconsider the game mechanics. The SPACE shot converts frames to white when the W bar points at them. But the W bar was on the LEFT side (pointing LEFT), and the SPACE shot hit the left blue square's border, converting it from charcoal to dark gray.
But the bottom frame was converted to white, not dark gray. So maybe the conversion depends on the frame's type or the W bar's direction.
Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just focus on converting the remaining frames. The top-right frame is still at rows 24-27, cols 44-47 (charcoal). The player is at rows 24-27, cols 33-35. The W bar is at col 32 (LEFT side).
To shoot at the top-right frame, I need the W bar to point RIGHT. But the W bar is on the LEFT side. So I need to switch the W bar to the RIGHT side.
But earlier, I did RIGHT and the W bar stayed on the LEFT side. Let me try RIGHT again to see if it switches the W bar to the RIGHT side.
Actually, wait. Looking at the ascii:
Row 24: `GGGGWNNNwwwwww`
The W bar is at col 32, and the player is at cols 33-35. The W bar is to the LEFT of the player. So the W bar is on the LEFT side.
But I did RIGHT, which should switch the W bar to the RIGHT side. Unless the W bar's side is determined by the direction of the last horizontal movement, and RIGHT means the W bar is on the RIGHT side of the player.
Wait, maybe the W bar's side is determined by the direction of the last horizontal movement, but the W bar is always on the side opposite to the movement direction. So RIGHT means the player moved RIGHT, and the W bar is on the LEFT side (opposite to the movement).
That would explain why the W bar is on the LEFT side after I did RIGHT. The player moved RIGHT, and the W bar is on the LEFT side.
So to get the W bar on the RIGHT side, I need to move LEFT. Let me try LEFT to switch the W bar to the RIGHT side, then SPACE to shoot at the top-right frame.
But wait, the top-right frame is to the RIGHT of the player. If the W bar is on the RIGHT side, it would point RIGHT, and the SPACE shot would hit the top-right frame.
Let me try LEFT, SPACE.

Hypothesis: W bar is on the side OPPOSITE to movement direction. So LEFT should put W bar on RIGHT side, pointing at top-right frame.

</parameter>
